contentBlocks.original:
प्रसन्नाङ्गरागं प्रभाकाञ्चनाङ्गं
जगद्भीतशौर्यं तुषाराद्रिधैर्यम् ।
तृणीभूतहेतिं रणोद्यद्विभूतिं
भजे वायुपुत्रं पवित्राप्तमित्रम् ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
prasannāṅgarāgaṃ prabhākāñchanāṅgaṃ
jagadbhītaśauryaṃ tuṣārādridhairyam ।
tṛṇībhūtahētiṃ raṇōdyadvibhūtiṃ
bhajē vāyuputraṃ pavitrāptamitram ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: With a pleasing bodily glow, with limbs radiant as gold, with courage that terrifies the world, with the steadfastness of the snow-mountain — treating weapons as mere straws, with the glory of rising battle blazing forth — I worship Hanumān.
contentBlocks.meanings: prasanna-aṅga-rāga: pleasing bodily glow | prabhā-kāñcana-aṅga: limbs radiant as gold | jagad-bhīta-śaurya: courage terrifying the world | tuṣāra-adri-dhairya: steadfastness of the snow-mountain (Himalaya) | tṛṇī-bhūta-heti: treating weapons as mere straws | raṇa-udyad-vibhūti: glory of rising battle

contentBlocks.original:
भजे पावनं भावना नित्यवासं
भजे बालभानु प्रभा चारुभासम् ।
भजे चन्द्रिका कुन्द मन्दार हासं
भजे सन्ततं रामभूपाल दासम् ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhajē pāvanaṃ bhāvanā nityavāsaṃ
bhajē bālabhānu prabhā chārubhāsam ।
bhajē chandrikā kunda mandāra hāsaṃ
bhajē santataṃ rāmabhūpāla dāsam ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: I worship the purifying one — who dwells eternally in the devotee's meditation — who shines with the beauty of the rising morning sun — whose smile is as white and gentle as moonlight, jasmine, and the mandāra flower — I worship the eternally celibate, ever-joyful Hanumān.
contentBlocks.meanings: pāvana: purifying one | bhāvanā-nitya-vāsa: dwelling eternally in meditation | bāla-bhānu-prabhā-cāru-bhāsa: shining with the beauty of the rising morning sun | candrikā-kunda-mandāra-hāsa: smile like moonlight, jasmine, mandāra flower | brahmacārī: celibate | ānanda: bliss/joy

contentBlocks.original:
भजे लक्ष्मणप्राणरक्षातिदक्षं
भजे तोषितानेक गीर्वाणपक्षम् ।
भजे घोर सङ्ग्राम सीमाहताक्षं
भजे रामनामाति सम्प्राप्तरक्षम् ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhajē lakṣmaṇaprāṇarakṣātidakṣaṃ
bhajē tōṣitānēka gīrvāṇapakṣam ।
bhajē ghōra saṅgrāma sīmāhatākṣaṃ
bhajē rāmanāmāti samprāptarakṣam ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: I worship the supremely skilled protector of Lakṣmaṇa's life — who pleased the entire assembly of the gods — whose eyes were steady in the fierce battle — I worship the glorious mountain-son, the servant of Kāruṇyarāma (the compassionate Rāma).
contentBlocks.meanings: lakṣmaṇa-prāṇa-rakṣā-ati-dakṣa: supremely skilled in protecting Lakṣmaṇa's life | toṣita-aneka-gīrvāṇa-pakṣa: pleased the entire assembly of gods | ghora-saṅgrāma-sīmā-hata-akṣa: eyes steady in the fierce battle | kāruṇya-rāma: the compassionate Rāma | giri-suta: son of the mountain (Añjanā was daughter of a mountain)

contentBlocks.original:
कृताभीलनाधक्षितक्षिप्तपादं
घनक्रान्त भृङ्गं कटिस्थोरु जङ्घम् ।
वियद्व्याप्तकेशं भुजाश्लेषिताश्मं
जयश्री समेतं भजे रामदूतम् ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kṛtābhīlanādhakṣitakṣiptapādaṃ
ghanakrānta bhṛṅgaṃ kaṭisthōru jaṅgham ।
viyadvyāptakēśaṃ bhujāślēṣitāśmaṃ
jayaśrī samētaṃ bhajē rāmadūtam ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: With feet deliberately placed upon the ground — with bees hovering around like a dark cloud — with hair filling the sky — with arms embracing mountains — I bow to the gigantic form of Hanumān who terrified the demons.
contentBlocks.meanings: kṛta-abhīla-nādha-kṣita-kṣipta-pāda: feet deliberately placed upon the ground with a fearful crash | ghana-krānta-bhṛṅga: bees hovering like a dark cloud | viyat-vyāpta-keśa: hair filling the sky | bhuja-āśleṣita-aśa: arms embracing the horizon | rākṣasa-trāsa: terror of the demons

contentBlocks.original:
चलद्वालघातं भ्रमच्चक्रवालं
कठोराट्टहासं प्रभिन्नाब्जजाण्डम् ।
महासिंहनादा द्विशीर्णत्रिलोकं
भजे चाञ्जनेयं प्रभुं वज्रकायम् ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chaladvālaghātaṃ bhramachchakravāḻaṃ
kaṭhōrāṭṭahāsaṃ prabhinnābjajāṇḍam ।
mahāsiṃhanādā dviśīrṇatrilōkaṃ
bhajē chāñjanēyaṃ prabhuṃ vajrakāyam ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: With his swinging tail dealing blows, whirling like a chakra — with his fearsome, thundering roar — bursting asunder the cosmic egg (brahmāṇḍa) — the great lion-roar splitting the three worlds in two — I bow to Hanumān.
contentBlocks.meanings: calat-vāla-ghāta: blow of the swinging tail | bhramat-cakra-vāla: whirling like a chakra | kaṭhora-aṭṭa-hāsa: fearsome thundering roar | prabhinna-abja-jāṇḍa: bursting asunder the cosmic egg (brahmāṇḍa) | mahā-siṃha-nāda: great lion-roar | dvi-śīrṇa-tri-loka: splitting the three worlds in two

contentBlocks.original:
रणे भीषणे मेघनादे सनादे
सरोषे समारोपणामित्र मुख्ये ।
खगानां घनानां सुराणां च मार्गे
नटन्तं समन्तं हनूमन्तमीडे ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
raṇē bhīṣaṇē mēghanādē sanādē
sarōṣē samārōpaṇāmitra mukhyē ।
khagānāṃ ghanānāṃ surāṇāṃ cha mārgē
naṭantaṃ samantaṃ hanūmantamīḍē ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: In the terrifying battlefield, roaring with the sound of Meghanāda (Indrajit's thunder-weapon) — with fierce, surging enemies mounted to the fore — dancing in the paths of birds, clouds, and gods — I bow to Hanumān who is beyond all.
contentBlocks.meanings: raṇe bhīṣaṇe: in the terrifying battlefield | meghanāde sanāde: roaring with Meghanāda's thunderous sound | saroṣe samāropaṇa-amitra-mukhye: fierce, surging enemies mounted to the fore | khagānāṃ ghanānāṃ surāṇāṃ ca mārge: in the paths of birds, clouds, and gods | naṭantaṃ: dancing

contentBlocks.original:
घनद्रत्न जम्भारि दम्भोलि भारं
घनद्दन्त निर्धूत कालोग्रदन्तम् ।
पदाघात भीताब्धि भूतादिवासं
रणक्षोणिदक्षं भजे पिङ्गलाक्षम् ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ghanadratna jambhāri dambhōḻi bhāraṃ
ghanaddanta nirdhūta kālōgradantam ।
padāghāta bhītābdhi bhūtādivāsaṃ
raṇakṣōṇidakṣaṃ bhajē piṅgaḻākṣam ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: With a dense jeweled thunderbolt from Indra (jambhāri), heavy as his pride — with massive, teeth-striking teeth that shook off even Kāla's terrible tooth — with the earth trembling at the blow of his foot — I bow to Rāma's servant, Hanumān.
contentBlocks.meanings: ghana-ratna-jambhāri-dambholi-bhāra: dense jeweled thunderbolt (vajra) heavy as pride | ghana-dat-nirdhūta-kāla-ugra-danta: massive teeth shaking off even fierce Kāla's tooth | padāghāta-bhīta-abdhi-bhūta-ādi-vāsa: earth trembling at his foot-blow | rāma-dāsa: servant of Rāma

contentBlocks.original:
महाग्राहपीडां महोत्पातपीडां
महारोगपीडां महातीव्रपीडाम् ।
हरत्यस्तु ते पादपद्मानुरक्तो
नमस्ते कपिश्रेष्ठ रामप्रियाय ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahāgrāhapīḍāṃ mahōtpātapīḍāṃ
mahārōgapīḍāṃ mahātīvrapīḍām ।
haratyastu tē pādapadmānuraktō
namastē kapiśrēṣṭha rāmapriyāya ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Great crocodile affliction, great calamity affliction, great disease affliction, great intense affliction — all these are destroyed for one who is devoted to your lotus-feet. Salutations to you, the formidable one.
contentBlocks.meanings: mahā-grāha-pīḍā: great crocodile (danger) affliction | mahā-utpāta-pīḍā: great calamity affliction | mahā-roga-pīḍā: great disease affliction | mahā-tīvra-pīḍā: great intense affliction | harati: destroys | pāda-padma-anurakta: devoted to the lotus-feet | namas te: salutations to you

contentBlocks.original:
जराभारतो भूरि पीडां शरीरे
निराधारणारूढ गाढ प्रतापी ।
भवत्पादभक्तिं भवद्भक्तिरक्तिं
कुरु श्रीहनूमत्प्रभो मे दयालो ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jarābhāratō bhūri pīḍāṃ śarīrē
nirādhāraṇārūḍha gāḍha pratāpī ।
bhavatpādabhaktiṃ bhavadbhaktiraktiṃ
kuru śrīhanūmatprabhō mē dayāḻō ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Bearing the heavy burden of old age afflicting the body — standing without any support, powerful and mighty — make me devoted to your feet, make me filled with devotion to you — please, O Śrī Hanumān.
contentBlocks.meanings: jarā-bhāra: burden of old age | bhūri-pīḍā: heavy affliction | śarīre: in the body | nirādhāra: without support | ārūḍha-gāḍha-pratāpī: powerfully mighty yet unsupported | bhavat-pāda-bhaktiṃ: devotion to your feet | bhavad-bhakti-raktiṃ: filled with devotion to you | śrī-hanumān: O Śrī Hanumān

contentBlocks.original:
महायोगिनो ब्रह्मरुद्रादयो वा
न जानन्ति तत्त्वं निजं राघवस्य ।
कथं ज्ञायते मादृशे नित्यमेव
प्रसीद प्रभो वानरेन्द्रो नमस्ते ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahāyōginō brahmarudrādayō vā
na jānanti tattvaṃ nijaṃ rāghavasya ।
kathaṃ jñāyatē mādṛśē nityamēva
prasīda prabhō vānarēndrō namastē ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Great yogīs, Brahma, Rudra, and others — even they do not know the inner truth of Śrī Rāghava (Rāma). How then can it be known by one like me who is always thus (ordinary)? Therefore I take refuge at the feet of Hanumān, who knows that truth.
contentBlocks.meanings: mahā-yogino: great yogīs | brahma-rudra-ādayo vā: Brahmā, Rudra, and others | na jānanti tattvaṃ nijaṃ rāghavasya: do not know the inner truth of Rāghava | kathaṃ jñāyate mādṛśe: how can it be known by one like me | pāda: feet | śaraṇam: refuge

contentBlocks.original:
नमस्ते महासत्त्ववाहाय तुभ्यं
नमस्ते महावज्रदेहाय तुभ्यम् ।
नमस्ते परीभूत सूर्याय तुभ्यं
नमस्ते कृतामर्त्य कार्याय तुभ्यम् ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namastē mahāsattvavāhāya tubhyaṃ
namastē mahāvajradēhāya tubhyam ।
namastē parībhūta sūryāya tubhyaṃ
namastē kṛtāmartya kāryāya tubhyam ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to you — bearer of great strength (mahā-sattva) — salutations to you — bearer of a body hard as the great thunderbolt — salutations to you — who has transcended (surpassed) the sun — salutations to you — the son of Añjanā.
contentBlocks.meanings: namas te: salutations | mahā-sattva-vāha: bearer of great essence/strength | mahā-vajra-deha: body hard as the great thunderbolt | parī-bhūta-sūrya: one who has transcended/surpassed the sun | añjanā-suta: son of Añjanā

contentBlocks.original:
नमस्ते सदा ब्रह्मचर्याय तुभ्यं
नमस्ते सदा वायुपुत्राय तुभ्यम् ।
नमस्ते सदा पिङ्गलाक्षाय तुभ्यं
नमस्ते सदा रामभक्ताय तुभ्यम् ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namastē sadā brahmacharyāya tubhyaṃ
namastē sadā vāyuputrāya tubhyam ।
namastē sadā piṅgaḻākṣāya tubhyaṃ
namastē sadā rāmabhaktāya tubhyam ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to you — always observing celibacy (brahmacarya) — salutations to you — always the son of Vāyu — salutations to you — always tawny-eyed (piṅgalākṣa) — salutations to you — always Rāma's servant.
contentBlocks.meanings: namas te: salutations | sadā brahmacarya: always observing celibacy | vāyu-putra: son of Vāyu (wind god) | piṅgala-akṣa: tawny/amber-eyed | rāma-dāsa: servant of Rāma

contentBlocks.original:
हनूमद्भुजङ्गप्रयातं प्रभाते
प्रदोषेऽपि वा चार्धरात्रेऽपि मर्त्यः ।
पठन्नश्नतोऽपि प्रमुक्तोघजालो
सदा सर्वदा रामभक्तिं प्रयाति ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hanūmadbhujaṅgaprayātaṃ prabhātē
pradōṣē'pi vā chārdharātrē'pi martyaḥ ।
paṭhannaśnatō'pi pramuktōghajālō
sadā sarvadā rāmabhaktiṃ prayāti ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: A mortal who recites this Hanumad-Bhujanga Prayāta [stotram] — in the morning, or at dusk, or even at midnight — even while eating — is freed from the entire net of great sins, and attains liberation (mukti).
contentBlocks.meanings: hanumad-bhujanga-prayātaṃ: this Hanumad Bhujanga Prayāta stotram | prabhāte: in the morning | pradoṣe: at dusk | ardha-rātre: at midnight | mṛtyu: mortal/human | paṭhan: reciting | aśnato'pi: even while eating | pra-mukta-ogha-jāla: freed from the entire net of great sins | mukti: liberation

contentBlocks.translation: Thus ends the Añjaneya Bhujanga Prayāta Stotram of the blessed Añjaneya.
contentBlocks.meanings: iti: thus ends | śrīmat-āñjaneya: of the blessed Añjaneya (Hanumān, son of Añjanā) | bhujanga-prayāta-stotram: the stotram in the bhujanga-prayāta (serpent-gliding) meter
